train_cars.definition: Colored plastic pieces (45 per player) placed on the board to claim routes between cities. Once placed, they remain for the rest of the game. When a player has 2 or fewer remaining, the final round triggers.

train_car_cards.definition: Cards in 8 colors (plus Locomotives) used to claim routes. To claim a route, play cards matching the route's color equal to its length. Drawn from the face-up display or the draw deck.

locomotive.definition: Wild card that substitutes for any color when claiming a route. 14 in the deck. When drawn face-up, it counts as both of your allowed draws. If 3 or more Locomotives appear in the face-up display, all 5 face-up cards are discarded and replaced.

destination_ticket.definition: A card showing two cities and a point value. If you connect those cities with a continuous path of your claimed routes by game end, you score the points. If not, you lose that many points. Kept secret until final scoring.

route.definition: A set of connected colored spaces on the board between two adjacent cities. Claimed by playing matching Train Car Cards equal to the route's length (1-6 spaces). Once claimed, no other player can use it.

double_route.definition: Two parallel routes connecting the same pair of cities. A single player cannot claim both. In 2-3 player games, once one double route is claimed, the other is permanently closed to all players.

face_up_cards.definition: 5 Train Car Cards placed face-up beside the board. Players may draw from these instead of the deck. Drawn cards are immediately replaced from the deck. If 3 or more are Locomotives, all 5 are discarded and replaced.

longest_continuous_path.definition: The longest unbroken chain of routes belonging to one player. May include loops and pass through the same city multiple times, but each plastic train piece counts only once. Worth a 10-point bonus at game end; tied players each receive the bonus.

scoring_marker.definition: Wooden piece placed on the scoring track around the board. Advanced immediately when a player claims a route, according to the route scoring table.
